notes: Two user turns in ONE live aider REPL session separated by a one-shot `/model <name>` slash that swaps the active main model MID-SESSION between them: turn 1 runs on model A, the user issues `/model openai/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored` (aider's cmd_model raises SwitchCoder(main_model=…) and the new coder writes a `> Main model: <name> …` blockquote to .aider.chat.history.md), then turn 2 runs on model B — the same model A→B mechanism as aider/model-switch, here under the canonical model-switch-midsession id. THE MODEL SWITCH ITSELF IS NOT EXPRESSIBLE IN THIS DSL: the events.jsonl lifecycle stream the validator reads carries no model_name field (the Phase struct has phase/expected_state/kind/max_delay_ms/duration_at_least_ms/same_session_as/new_session/invariants and nothing model-related). So this spec asserts only the TWO-TURN MID-SESSION LIFECYCLE SKELETON on ONE session — ready→working→ready twice on the SAME session_id, no waiting ever, a single process_exited at teardown, proving the switch happened DURING one live session in place (not via a restart). The model A→B change (ModelName follows latest-wins to model B; TotalTokens accumulates across the switch) is asserted out-of-band: at the metrics-level summary.model_name in the .replay.json.golden (diffed by replay-fixtures) plus the aider parser unit test TestParser_MainModel_AfterSlashCommand (parser_test.go:214), which drives this recipe's exact `> Main model: openai/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored …` line and asserts the post-switch turn carries model B — NOT this file. SESSION IDENTITY (aider-specific, differs from pi/codex): aider stores its transcript as .aider.chat.history.md in the cwd (FilesUnderCWD), so the daemon keys the WHOLE session by proc-<PID> — there is NO separate transcript-UUID rollout session and no handoff/reconcile phase. Every event (the state arc, pid_discovered, process_exited) lands on the one proc-<PID> row, so the turn arcs, pid_bind, and teardown all pin to session_birth via same_session_as — this is what proves MID-SESSION continuity (one session_id from birth to teardown across the switch). LAZY TRANSCRIPT: .aider.chat.history.md materializes only once the first user input lands, so the live REPL is observable from launch as the proc-<PID> pre-session born ready (the process scanner) before the per-cwd transcript appears — session_birth, which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch', matches that pre-session born ready. AIDER TURN-END IS SYNTHESIZED, NOT MARKED: the markdown transcript has no turn-end marker — a `> Tokens:` line closes one model CALL (assistant_message), not the turn. End-of-turn is synthesized OUT-OF-BAND by IdleFlush after aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter (~1.5s) quiet, clamped to the SessionDetector's ~5s working-refresh poll, so the EFFECTIVE working→ready latency after a turn's last line is ~5s. The recipe therefore uses generous inter-turn / trailing sleeps (≥7s) so each turn's working→ready flip fires (IdleFlush) AND is observed (next poll) before the next prompt re-enters working; with shorter sleeps the next `####` prompt lands before IdleFlush and the parser opens the new turn WITHOUT an intervening turn_done — the per-turn reset is lost (TestParser_NewUserPromptAfterMultiCall_StaysWorking). WHY interactive (not headless `aider --message`): the `/model` slash only exists inside the REPL and the scenario needs two turns; the aider interactive driver keeps the REPL alive across both turns and kills the tmux session once at the end → a SINGLE process_exited. WHY NO wait_turn after the `/model` slash: the driver's `slash` step bumps the expected-turn counter like `send`, but `/model` writes a `> Main model:` blockquote and produces NO `> Tokens:` close (no assistant turn) — so a wait_turn placed after it would hang on the unchanged turn count; the recipe covers the switch with a fixed sleep instead. SINGLE SESSION: a mid-session model switch does NOT create a new session, so there is exactly one proc-<PID> session_id and one process_exited. HOW runs differ: the concrete model values are environment-dependent (issue #440 — assert presence/change, not a hardcoded pair); exact assistant wording, per-turn token counts, PID, and sub-second timing vary; the working→ready edge lands with ~5s effective idle-flush latency (a timing delay, not a correctness gap). Stable structurally: two ready→working→ready arcs on ONE proc-<PID> session, a single process_exited, no waiting, and a final ready state — plus (golden-level, not here) a non-empty summary.model_name equal to model B. NOTE: this spec is the DOCUMENTED TARGET — validated when aider auth is available in-env (no provider key present at authoring time; headless aider hangs on OpenRouter OAuth).

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch (the proc-<PID> pre-session; the lazy transcript has not materialized yet)
First prompt → working on model A (binds the live aider session; the pre-session never enters working)
First turn ends on model A → back to ready (IdleFlush synthesizes turn-end after the prompt goes quiet); state never entered waiting
After the mid-session /model switch, the second prompt → working again on the SAME session, now on model B
Second turn ends on model B → back to ready; state never entered waiting
PID bound to the live aider session
REPL torn down once after the second turn → a single process_exited on the session; state immediately before teardown is ready
